issues.detail: Annotation for 'Sie' has lemma 'Sie' with pos 'noun' and gloss_hint 'she (pronoun)' — 'Sie' (formal you) is a pronoun, not a noun

Example is under sense 9 (design/Plan/Entwurf) but annotation gloss_hint for Anlage says 'disposition' (sense 0). Context is about a bad plan/design where the execution could not improve. Correct gloss_hint should be 'design'
gloss_hint for Dreier is 'three-point shot' but this example is under sense 1 (number three). Correct gloss_hint should be 'number three'
Annotation for 'Sie' has lemma 'she', pos 'noun', gloss_hint 'trumpet solo' — completely wrong. 'Sie' here is formal you (pronoun). Only one annotation present, missing all other content words

gloss_hint for Loch is 'hole' but this example appears under both sense 4 (orifice) and sense 6 (opening/door/window). For 'Mach's Loch zu!' (shut your trap/shut the door), hint should match the intended sense not the physical hole sense
All annotations are on English translation text (Children, who, are, susceptible, pathogens, holes, defense, etc.) instead of German source text. Entire annotation set is invalid
gloss_hint for Stift is 'pen' but this example is under sense 2 (rod — elongated metal object). Should be 'rod'. Translation says 'pointless cylindrical pin' which is correct for the rod sense
lemma for Stift is lowercase 'stift' — should be capitalized 'Stift' (it is a noun). gloss_hint is 'tree\'s trunk' which is not a valid gloss_en substring
All annotations are on English translation text (their, grandmother, basket, full, of, bright, red, apples) instead of German source text. Entire annotation set is invalid

Multiple annotations have English lemmas: Buch has lemma 'book', Korb has lemma 'basket', Päckchen has lemma 'package', Bücher has lemma 'book'. Lemmas must be German dictionary forms: Buch, Korb, Päckchen, Buch

Multiple annotations have lowercase German lemmas: Truhe lemma is 'truh' (should be 'Truhe'), geflochtenem lemma is 'geflecht' (should be 'geflochten'), Korb lemma is 'korb' (should be 'Korb'), Trödelmarkt lemma is 'trödelmarkt' (should be 'Trödelmarkt')
This example is under sense 10 (metal hand guard) but annotation gloss_hint for Korb says 'basket'. Should match 'metal hand guard'. Translation also incorrectly says 'basket' instead of 'hand guard'
This example is under sense 9 (wire face guard) but annotation gloss_hint for Korb says 'basket'. Should match 'wire face guard'
First annotation 'Sein' has lemma 'sein' with pos 'verb' and gloss_hint 'be (copula)' but 'Sein' here is a possessive pronoun meaning 'his', not the verb 'sein'
All annotations are on English translation text (long-awaited, film, finally, out) instead of German source text. Entire annotation set is invalid
Annotation for 'heraus' has pos 'adjective' but heraus is an adverb. Should be pos 'adverb'
Annotation has form 'that age' (English) with lemma 'Alter|noun'. Form should be from German text ('Alter'), lemma should be just 'Alter'
Annotation for 'Reich' has lemma 'reich' (adjective, wealthy) but 'Reich' here is the imperative form of 'reichen' (to pass/hand). Should be lemma 'reichen', pos 'verb'
All annotations are on English translation text (I, need, a, key, to, understand, the, message) instead of German source text. Entire annotation set is invalid
Annotation for 'Bauch' has English lemma 'belly' — should be German lemma 'Bauch'
Annotations have English lemmas: Bauch has lemma 'belly' (should be 'Bauch'), Vase has lemma 'vase' (should be 'Vase'). Duplicate Bauch annotation
All annotations have English lemmas: Bauch='belly', Fähre='ferry', Autos='auto', Lastkraftwagen='lastkraftwagen'. Should be German: Bauch, Fähre, Auto, Lastkraftwagen
Annotation for 'Bauch' has English lemma 'belly' — should be 'Bauch'
Annotations have English lemmas: Bauch='belly' (x2), Höhe='height', Geschwindigkeit='speed'. Should all be German dictionary forms
Annotations have English lemmas: Anlage='facility', Bauch='belly', Boden='ground', Koffer='suitcase', Stunde='hour'. Should all be German dictionary forms
Annotation for 'Bauch' has English lemma 'belly' — should be 'Bauch'
Translation is null. German: 'Ein bestimmter Hafen in Südvietnam soll erfolgreich gegen feindliche Froschmänner von Delphinen verteidigt worden sein, denen man das Töten beigebracht hatte.'
Translation says 'the holes in the baskets' but German 'die löchrigen Körbe' means 'the holey/damaged baskets' — the baskets are löchrig (full of holes), not 'she saw holes in the baskets'
Example is for sense 10 (metal hand guard). Translation says 'The basket got a scratch' but Korb here means hand guard. Should be 'The hand guard got a scratch, but that is what it was there for.'
Example is for sense 9 (wire face guard). Translation says 'She pushed the basket upward' but Korb here means wire face guard. Should be 'She pushed the face guard up and glanced at the referees.'
